title: Deep caves and giant trees lane
summary: Thirtieth structured packet for Eco Explorer. Lane 3 is a vertical-exploration lane focused on expanding caves and giant-tree climbing into deeper, more magical but still science-forward sub-ecosystems. The lane starts with a traversal-runtime split, then builds one giant-tree old-growth pocket, one deeper cave chapter, and finally one optional bridge that makes the two feel like a coherent family.

execution_order.goal: Prepare the deep-cave and giant-tree runtime split.
execution_order.why_now: Lane 3 needs one realistic runtime foundation before it builds bigger spaces.

execution_order.goal: Expand traversal runtime for giant trees and deeper caves.
execution_order.why_now: The current proofs need a little more vertical budget before they can become real chapters.

execution_order.goal: Review the giant-tree and deep-cave runtime expansion.
execution_order.why_now: The lane should only proceed if the runtime remains readable and maintainable.

execution_order.goal: Prepare the first giant-tree old-growth pocket.
execution_order.why_now: The most attractive next vertical chapter is a huge old-growth climbable space.

execution_order.goal: Implement the first giant-tree old-growth pocket.
execution_order.why_now: A big-tree chapter is the fastest way to make vertical exploration feel new and memorable.

execution_order.goal: Review the first giant-tree old-growth pocket.
execution_order.why_now: Scale readability and traversal friendliness are the main risk in giant-tree spaces.

execution_order.goal: Prepare the first deeper cave sub-ecosystem chapter.
execution_order.why_now: The cave chapter should be planned as a real layered ecosystem rather than just more tunnel length.

execution_order.goal: Implement the first deeper cave sub-ecosystem chapter.
execution_order.why_now: Deeper caves are the second anchor of the vertical-exploration family.

execution_order.goal: Review the first deeper cave chapter.
execution_order.why_now: The cave pass should stay cozy, readable, and worth revisiting.

execution_order.goal: Connect giant-tree and deep-cave exploration through one optional wilderness bridge.
execution_order.why_now: One light connective move will make the lane feel like a family instead of two isolated experiments.

execution_order.goal: Review the first vertical-exploration family pass.
execution_order.why_now: This is the quality gate for whether lane 3 should keep expanding from this base.

guardrails: Do not add combat, fail-state cave danger, or a survival loop.
Do not demand strict geographic accuracy; use grounded scale cheats where they help the game.
Keep vertical traversal readable and kid-friendly at the current screen size.
Treat caves and giant trees as science-forward sub-ecosystems, not as generic fantasy dungeons or platform towers.
acceptance_focus: Lane 3 creates real vertical exploration depth instead of only taller geometry.
Giant trees and deeper caves feel distinct, readable, and fun.
The vertical-exploration family becomes a stable future growth lane.
handoff_prompt: Read AGENTS.md, .agents/lane-runner.md, .agents/project-memory.md, .agents/work-queue.md, .agents/lanes/lane-3.md, docs/reports/2026-03-30-deep-caves-and-giant-trees-lane.md, and this packet. Treat lane 3 as the vertical-exploration lane. Build a realistic but playful path from the current cave and climbing proofs into deeper cave chapters and giant-tree exploration while staying cozy, readable, and science-forward.
